note: The Stage 1 proving run, 18 Jul 2026, $0.000591. Thin truncate: passes its test, does not reject a negative length. The judge returned BLOCK and named the right criterion. Kept because a case the judge got right is the baseline that tells you when it stops getting it right.

labels.text: truncate rejects a negative length with a clear error
labels.why: there is no guard at all. A negative length slices from the end, so truncate('hello', -2) returns 'hel' silently instead of raising.

labels.text: the result never exceeds length characters
labels.why: text[:length] is bounded by length for any non-negative length; nothing is appended after the slice.
